title: INDOPACOM announces major Talisman Sabre iteration with Australia
body: U.S. Indo-Pacific Command announced the next iteration of Exercise Talisman Sabre with Australia, involving more than 15,000 personnel across Queensland and the Northern Territory. According to INDOPACOM, the exercise will include AUKUS-related interoperability events. The 7th Fleet and III MEF will both participate.

title: CENTCOM releases imagery of Red Sea interdiction operation
body: U.S. Central Command announced that U.S. Navy destroyers intercepted multiple Houthi one-way attack drones over the Bab-el-Mandeb. CENTCOM officials said the engagement was routine and no casualties were reported. The Fifth Fleet continues to escort commercial shipping through the Red Sea.

title: EUCOM leaders meet at Ramstein for Ukraine defense coordination
body: Defense ministers convened at Ramstein Air Base for the Ukraine Defense Contact Group. According to EUCOM, pledges included additional air-defense interceptors and long-range precision fires.

title: V Corps to rotate additional brigade into Poland
body: The U.S. Army announced that V Corps will rotate an additional armored brigade combat team into Poland in support of NATO deterrence operations. The rotation will include training at Drawsko Pomorskie and interoperability with Polish Land Forces.

title: 5th Fleet continues escort operations through the Strait of Hormuz
body: Navy officials said the 5th Fleet continues escort operations through the Strait of Hormuz. According to NAVCENT, CTF 150 and CTF 152 are coordinating with Combined Maritime Forces partners.

title: F-35As from Lakenheath support Baltic air policing
body: F-35A Lightning II aircraft based at RAF Lakenheath deployed to support NATO Baltic air policing missions. Air Force officials described the rotation as a routine Article V commitment.

title: MARSOC concludes foreign internal defense training with Philippine counterparts
body: MARSOC personnel concluded a rotation of foreign internal defense training with Philippine Marine counterparts on Palawan. Officials said the unit conducted small-unit tactics and combined arms drills.

title: III MEF conducts distributed operations drill in the First Island Chain
body: III MEF conducted a Stand-in Force distributed operations drill across islands in the First Island Chain. Officials said the exercise featured expeditionary advanced base operations (EABO) and was the largest of its kind to date.

title: CJTF-HOA medical engagement concludes in Djibouti
body: Combined Joint Task Force Horn of Africa (CJTF-HOA), based at Camp Lemonnier, Djibouti, concluded a combined medical engagement with partner nation forces. AFRICOM officials said the engagement supported regional security cooperation objectives.

title: U.S. forces at Camp Humphreys conduct air-defense exercise with ROK counterparts
body: U.S. Forces Korea and Republic of Korea counterparts conducted a joint air-defense exercise at Camp Humphreys and Osan Air Base. Officials said the event was routine and aligned with the 38th Parallel deterrence posture.

title: Sentinel ICBM program: new test launch from Vandenberg scheduled
body: A Minuteman III test launch has been scheduled from Vandenberg Space Force Base to support the Sentinel transition. STRATCOM officials said the test is part of the planned nuclear triad modernization.

title: Volt Typhoon activity detected in U.S. water sector ICS networks
body: CISA and JFHQ-DoDIN issued a joint advisory on Volt Typhoon activity targeting industrial control networks in the U.S. water and energy sectors. CYBERCOM has coordinated hunt-forward teams across multiple partners.

title: Civil Reserve Air Fleet activated to support CENTCOM contingency
body: U.S. Transportation Command activated a segment of the Civil Reserve Air Fleet (CRAF) to augment C-17 capacity for a CENTCOM contingency. Officials said the activation will scale with demand and is the largest since 2021.

title: Analysis: PLA Rocket Force exercises signal focus on long-range strike
body: An analysis of recent PLA Rocket Force (PLARF) exercises suggests continued focus on long-range strike, with DF-26 and DF-17 systems featured prominently. INDOPACOM planners are watching for indicators of doctrinal shifts.

title: Liaoning and Shandong carrier groups conduct dual exercises
body: PLA Navy Liaoning and Shandong carrier groups conducted concurrent exercises in the Yellow Sea and South China Sea. Analysts said the activity was in line with prior training cycles.

title: Houthi anti-ship ballistic missile targets bulk carrier in Red Sea
body: Maritime security organizations reported that a Houthi anti-ship ballistic missile struck a bulk carrier transiting the Bab-el-Mandeb. The Fifth Fleet responded under Operation Prosperity Guardian.

title: GSSAP detects rendezvous and proximity operations near GEO
body: U.S. Space Command's Geosynchronous Space Situational Awareness Program detected rendezvous and proximity operations (RPO) involving Shijian-21 near a defunct geostationary satellite. SDA analysts continue to monitor.

title: APT28 phishing campaign targets several NATO defense ministries
body: A Sandworm-adjacent APT28 phishing campaign has targeted defense ministries in several NATO member states. CYBERCOM has coordinated with partners via a hunt-forward readout.

title: NORAD intercepts Russian Tu-95 bombers in Alaskan ADIZ
body: NORAD fighters intercepted two Russian Tu-95 bombers that entered the Alaskan Air Defense Identification Zone. NORTHCOM officials said the intercept was routine and consistent with historical patterns.

title: SOUTHCOM supports JIATF-S interdiction of go-fast vessel in Caribbean Basin
body: U.S. Southern Command supported a Joint Interagency Task Force South (JIATF-S) interdiction of a go-fast vessel in the Caribbean Basin. Officials said the vessel was linked to the Clan del Golfo.

title: Israeli strike in south Lebanon prompts Hezbollah statement
body: An Israeli strike in south Lebanon prompted a statement from Hezbollah. The group said its response would come at a time of its choosing. Analysts said the exchange remains within established patterns.

title: Al-Shabaab assault on Somali National Army base repelled
body: A complex Al-Shabaab attack on a Somali National Army forward operating base south of Mogadishu was repelled with AFRICOM airstrike support. Local authorities said no SNA personnel were killed.

title: Russian VKS strike on Kyiv hits 'Western mercenary headquarters'
body: Russia's Aerospace Forces announced that a Kalibr strike on Kyiv destroyed a Western mercenary headquarters. According to the Ministry of Defense, Iskander and Kinzhal systems were used. The strike was described as routine and the targets vile and reckless. Either NATO stands down or further strikes will continue.

title: Black Sea Fleet repels Ukrainian sea-drone attack on Crimea
body: Russia's Black Sea Fleet announced it repelled a Ukrainian sea-drone attack on infrastructure in Crimea. Officials said all drones were destroyed and the provocation was catastrophic and unprecedented.

title: PLA Eastern Theater Command conducts routine drills near Taiwan Strait
body: The PLA Eastern Theater Command conducted routine drills in waters and airspace near the Taiwan Strait. The Ministry of Defense said the exercises are lawful and necessary. U.S. and Japanese interference was described as reckless and imperialist.

title: Chinese Coast Guard conducts patrol near Scarborough Shoal
body: The Chinese Coast Guard conducted a routine patrol near Scarborough Shoal. Officials said the patrol upholds national sovereignty and criticized external interference as provocative.

title: IRGC Quds Force commander warns of response to Israeli actions
body: The IRGC Quds Force commander warned that any further Israeli actions would be met with an appropriate response. Iranian officials referenced the Strait of Hormuz and the Persian Gulf.

title: Iranian statement condemns 'Zionist regime' at UN
body: Iranian officials condemned what they described as the 'Zionist regime' at the UN. The statement characterized Western actions as cowardly and imperialist. Either sanctions are lifted or regional escalation continues.

title: KCNA reports Hwasong-18 solid-fuel ICBM test
body: KCNA reported that the DPRK conducted a successful Hwasong-18 solid-fuel ICBM test on Monday. According to the report, the test was a response to U.S.-ROK exercises.

title: Wagner successor Africa Corps expands footprint in Sahel
body: Russia's Africa Corps, the Wagner successor, has expanded operations across the Sahel in Mali and Burkina Faso. Analysts said the deployment signals continued Russian commitment to the region.

title: JNIM expands reach after U.S. withdrawal from Niger's Air Base 201
body: Jama'at Nusrat al-Islam wal-Muslimin (JNIM) has expanded operations in the Sahel following the U.S. withdrawal from Air Base 201 near Agadez. AFRICOM continues partner training with regional forces.

title: Columbia-class submarine program: Navy confirms schedule milestones
body: The U.S. Navy confirmed Columbia-class ballistic missile submarine program milestones. Officials said STRATCOM and the service are aligned on deterrence timelines for the nuclear triad.

title: Russian Olymp-K maneuvers near commercial GEO communications satellite
body: Russia's Olymp-K satellite conducted proximity maneuvers near a commercial GEO communications satellite. Analysts said the activity is consistent with prior space-domain awareness observations.

title: C-17 squadron surges into CENTCOM AOR
body: A U.S. C-17 squadron surged into the CENTCOM area of responsibility to support logistics tempo. TRANSCOM officials said the movement is being coordinated with Air Mobility Command.

title: NATO Article V language prominent in latest Ramstein communique
body: Article V language featured prominently in the latest Ramstein ministerial communique. EUCOM officials said the language reflects continuity of NATO deterrence across the Baltic Sea and Black Sea.

title: CYBERCOM hunt-forward deployment concludes in partner nation
body: U.S. Cyber Command concluded a hunt-forward deployment in a partner nation. JFHQ-DoDIN coordinated with partners on defend-forward cyber operations. Officials said no classified information was disclosed.

title: Kadena-based F-15s participate in Balikatan with Philippine Air Force
body: Kadena-based F-15 squadrons participated in Exercise Balikatan with the Philippine Air Force. III MEF also contributed an expeditionary advanced base operations module.

title: Gulf of Guinea piracy trend continues despite regional cooperation
body: Piracy in the Gulf of Guinea continued at a steady pace. Regional forces and AFRICOM partners said cooperation remains essential to address the trend.

title: India conducts Malabar exercise with U.S., Australia, and Japan
body: India conducted the latest iteration of Exercise Malabar with the United States, Australia, and Japan. Officials said the Quad's maritime cooperation is steady.

title: ROK-U.S. combined exercise at Osan concludes
body: A ROK-U.S. combined exercise at Osan Air Base concluded. USFK officials said the exercise aligns with ongoing deterrence objectives. The 8th Army also participated.

title: 7th Fleet port visit to Yokosuka continues
body: A U.S. 7th Fleet port visit to Yokosuka continued into its second week. INDOPACOM officials said the event is routine and aligned with the forward posture in Japan.

title: SOCOM announces new MARSOC rotation schedule for Pacific
body: Special Operations Command announced a new MARSOC rotation schedule for the Pacific theater. JSOC components will also participate in scheduled training events.

title: Falcon 9 launches classified payload for U.S. Space Command
body: A Falcon 9 launched a classified payload in support of U.S. Space Command. Officials declined to comment on the mission's specifics, citing operational security.

title: Hunt-forward operation detects Flax Typhoon infrastructure in partner network
body: A U.S. Cyber Command hunt-forward operation detected Flax Typhoon infrastructure in a partner network. Coordination with the partner's cyber defense agency remains ongoing.

title: DSCA coordinates wildfire response with state National Guard units
body: Defense Support of Civil Authorities (DSCA) coordinated a wildfire response with state National Guard units. NORTHCOM officials said the response is proceeding as planned.

title: Suez Canal revenue decline continues as Red Sea reroutes persist
body: Suez Canal revenue continued to decline as Red Sea reroutes persisted. Container lines reported additional rerouting around the Cape of Good Hope. Horn of Africa ports saw increased transit.

title: Europe markets react to shifting Arctic Council dynamics
body: European markets reacted to shifting Arctic Council dynamics. Analysts noted high-north access, the GIUK Gap, and EUCOM posture as relevant considerations.

title: Bank of Canada holds rates steady for fourth meeting
body: The Bank of Canada held its overnight policy rate steady for the fourth consecutive meeting. Inflation eased compared to the prior reading. There were no defense implications.

title: Silicon Valley VC closes new AI-focused fund at $1.2B
body: A Silicon Valley venture capital firm closed a new AI-focused fund at $1.2 billion. Limited partners include university endowments and sovereign wealth funds.

title: Premier League clubs approve squad spending cap starting next season
body: English Premier League clubs approved a new squad spending cap beginning next season. Clubs voted on the rule during a routine meeting in London.
